mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor demand and rising household income, which drives consumer spending and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 137258.0 thousand as of July 2012
regime_hint: post-recession recovery with sustained employment gains
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive payroll months fail to produce significantly higher forward returns than negative payroll months (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 1.0% and remains there for 3+ consecutive months, the zero-lower-bound constraint becomes binding, signaling exhausted conventional monetary policy and elevated tail risk, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 5.25% as of July 2012 indicates room above ZLB
regime_hint: zero-lower-bound monetary regime with policy exhaustion
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC > 0.02 in the opposite direction.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) over 2 consecutive months signals weakening aggregate demand and consumer confidence, which reduces forward earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline month-over-month for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 370418.0M and HOUST at 1642.0K as of July 2012
regime_hint: late-cycle demand deceleration or early-stage contraction
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite joint RSAFS-HOUST contraction, or the correlation between the joint signal and forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerates above its 6-month moving average, signaling expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side strength, which supports corporate profit margins and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth > 6-month moving average growth rate
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window, or INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 5-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) rises above 2.5% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below 1.0%, the real policy rate becomes deeply negative, signaling financial repression that inflates asset prices and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 202.8 and FEDFUNDS at 5.25% as of August 2012
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect (negative real rates) shows no significant positive correlation with forward returns (p > 0.12).

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 4.5% and remains elevated for 2+ months, signaling labor market slack and reduced wage pressure, which lowers corporate labor costs and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: post-recession labor market with persistent slack
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 2.0% annualized in consecutive quarters while retail sales (RSAFS) contract, signaling a demand-side recession that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth < 2.0% for 2 consecutive quarters AND RSAFS declines
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or the joint signal shows no significant negative correlation with forward returns (p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines for 2 consecutive months while the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 4.5%, signaling simultaneous supply contraction and labor market deterioration, which reduces earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines month-over-month for 2 consecutive months AND UNRATE > 4.5%
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 111.9282 and UNRATE at 4.6% as of July 2012
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 58% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect between production decline and elevated unemployment shows no significant negative correlation with forward returns (p > 0.12).
